templates.original_text: Despite any contrary sentence, the limitation in Section 16 does not apply to claims under Section 13 or Section 14.
templates.mutated_text: Despite any contrary sentence, all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16.

templates.original_text: {vendor_role} will defend and indemnify {customer_role} against third-party claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights or that {vendor_role}'s performance violates applicable law.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} will defend {customer_role} against third-party claims only to the extent finally determined by a court to arise from {vendor_role}'s willful misconduct.

templates.original_text: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, including project-specific tools, frameworks, and know-how developed in the course of performance.
templates.mutated_text: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, but excludes Vendor Methods created, refined, or reduced to practice during the engagement.

templates.original_text: "Security Incident" means any suspected or actual unauthorized access to, acquisition of, use of, loss of, or disclosure of Customer Data.
templates.mutated_text: "Security Incident" means confirmed outbound extraction of Customer Data that is evidenced by a completed written forensic report approved by {vendor_role}'s incident response lead.

templates.original_text: {vendor_role} will notify {customer_role} without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours after discovering a Security Incident.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} will give written alert within {notice_period} after its incident lead confirms a Security Incident.

templates.original_text: {vendor_role} will process Customer Data only to provide and support the services, to comply with {customer_role}'s documented instructions, and as otherwise expressly authorized in an Order Form.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} may process Customer Data to provide and improve the services, develop analytics, and perform other business purposes reasonably related to the service relationship.

templates.original_text: If {customer_role} ends an Order Form without {vendor_role} default, {customer_role} will pay only accrued, undisputed fees and approved reimbursable expenses incurred before the effective date.
templates.mutated_text: If {customer_role} ends an Order Form without {vendor_role} default, {customer_role} will pay an exit charge equal to the full unpaid balance for that Order Form.

templates.original_text: Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and will not automatically extend unless {customer_role} signs a renewal order or gives written approval for a renewal period of one year or less.
templates.mutated_text: Each Order Form has the initial term stated in it and then continues for additional periods of {renewal_years} years each unless either party sends non-continuation notice at least {notice_days} days before the current period ends.

templates.original_text: This disclaimer does not limit {vendor_role}'s confidentiality duties, data handling duties, indemnities, service-level commitments, or remedies expressly stated in this Agreement.
templates.mutated_text: This disclaimer applies despite any express warranty, service commitment, indemnity, confidentiality obligation, data handling obligation, or remedy stated elsewhere in this Agreement.

templates.original_text: No amendment, modification, or waiver is effective unless in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} may modify service terms, acceptable use limits, and other online policies by posting an updated version to its website, effective upon posting.

templates.original_text: Neither party may assign this Agreement without the other party's prior written consent, except to an affiliate or successor in connection with a merger, reorganization, or sale of substantially all assets if the assignee assumes the assigning party's obligations.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} may assign this Agreement or delegate performance to any successor, affiliate, subcontractor, or financing source without {customer_role}'s consent.

templates.original_text: {vendor_role} will maintain commercially reasonable insurance at its own expense, including technology errors and omissions/cyber liability coverage of at least $5,000,000 per claim and commercial general liability coverage of at least $2,000,000 per occurrence.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} will maintain insurance it determines is commercially reasonable, including general liability coverage of at least ${gl_limit} per occurrence if available on commercially reasonable terms.

templates.original_text: Upon reasonable notice, {customer_role} or its independent auditor may review {vendor_role}'s records, systems, and controls relevant to the services, security, privacy, and compliance no more than once per year, and more frequently after a material incident or reasonable cause.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} will provide its then-current standard security summary upon written request no more than once per year.

templates.original_text: {vendor_role} may use affiliates, hosting providers, implementation contractors, and other service partners to perform portions of the services, provided {vendor_role} remains responsible for their work and binds each service partner to written obligations at least as protective as {vendor_role}'s obligations for Customer Data, confidentiality, security, and service performance.
templates.mutated_text: {vendor_role} may use affiliates, hosting providers, implementation contractors, and other service partners to perform portions of the services, provided {vendor_role} remains responsible for their work as if performed by {vendor_role}.
